title: Paginated Data Table
description:
Build a paginated data table that fetches one page of results at a time from the API.

### Requirements

- Fetch the first page on mount with `?page=1&limit=10`
- Display page navigation: Previous, page numbers, Next
- Disable Previous on first page, Next on last page
- Show total results count and current page range (e.g., "11–20 of 100")
- Show loading state while fetching each page (keep old data visible but dimmed)
- Cache previously fetched pages so going back is instant
- Handle errors per page fetch

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Table body | `table-body` |
| Row | `row-{index}` |
| Previous button | `prev-btn` |
| Next button | `next-btn` |
| Page button | `page-{num}` |
| Page info | `page-info` |
| Loading overlay | `loading-overlay` |
constraints:
- Must fetch pages from API (not client-side pagination)
- Previous button disabled on page 1
- Next button disabled on last page
- Must cache fetched pages
- Loading state must not remove existing data
hints: Store a cache as a ref: `Map<number, Row[]>`. Before fetching, check if the page exists in cache.
For the loading overlay, use a semi-transparent div over the table with `pointer-events: none`.
Calculate total pages from the API response: `Math.ceil(totalCount / limit)`.
